subject: Re: Weird-ordering of tilt-images
body:
This is awesome!

Thanks, Pranav :) 

On Monday, June 20, 2022 at 6:05:56 PM UTC-7 p.sha...@gmail.com wrote:

> Good you sorted it out. But just in case you want to be more
> systematic and less prone to error you can run this script.
> Note that it will preserve your original spoofed mdoc and write
> timestamps to a new one.
> To run
> python change_time_stamp.py before.mdoc after.mdoc
> Best,
> Pranav
> --
> Pranav Shah
> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>
> Division of Structural Biology,
> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
> University of Oxford,
> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
> UK
>
> On Tue, Jun 21, 2022 at 1:08 AM Digvijay Singh <digvij...@gmail.com> 
> wrote:
> >
> > [1] I dug a little further and copy-pasted the same tilt-images' 
> filenames into another mdoc file, then the order read by WARP was correct.
> >
> > [2] So the WARP is not seemingly reading the order from SubFramePath 
> field of the mdocs but DateTime field. The mdoc spoofed by the mdocspoofer 
> contains the same value for all DateTime fields which may have been causing 
> the WARP to read the order via some other way causing the order to go 
> haywire.
> >
> > Cheers,
> > Digvijay
> > On Sunday, June 19, 2022 at 9:37:34 PM UTC-7 Digvijay Singh wrote:
> >>
> >> Hi everyone
> >>
> >> I used Alister's mdocspoofer to spoof an mdoc for a particular set of 
> tilt-images. The resultant mdoc is totally fine. It lists the tilt-images 
> in the order specified in the _00n suffix in tilt-images' names.
> >>
> >> But when Warp reads them, the order seems off. Please see attached a 
> screenshot. The cumulative dose from +39 to +36 tilt-angle jumps from 2 to 
> 114 e-/A2, whereas, it should increase from 2 to 6 e-/A2 as per the mdoc ( 
> the one spoofed above & also attached).
> >>
> >> In my other set of data using docs from serial em where I store 
> tilt-images' frames with date-time stamps encoded by serialEM, the WARP 
> reads the tilt-series acquisition order perfectly. So I had assumed that 
> WARP reads the tilt-series acquisition order from the name in the 
> SubFramePath.
> >>
> >> Ofcourse, this incorrect reading of the order messes up the 3D-CTF 
> model.
> >>
> >> Does anyone know how exactly does the WARP read the order of 
> tilt-series acquisition from the mdoc file and/or what could be an issue 
> here? The WARP I am using is 1.0.9
> >>
> >> Thanks and cheers,
> >> Digvijay
> >>
> >>
> >>
> > --
> > You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google 
> Groups "Warp" group.
> > To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send 
> an email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.

> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/a1f0cf53-ccda-4a5a-b81b-4b6afc5aed60n%40googlegroups.com
> .
>
